finding.root_cause_summary: Base64Handler.encode() at line 16 calls string.getBytes() and decode() at line 20 calls new String(bytes) without specifying a charset, both relying on the platform default charset. When the runtime default charset differs between JDK 17 (likely UTF-8) and JDK 21 environments, non-ASCII characters like "äöüß" are corrupted during the round-trip encode→decode, causing the parameterized test to fail on all three inputs.

cited_files.detail: Line 16: string.getBytes() missing charset; Line 20: new String(bytes) missing charset
Line 11: @ValueSource includes non-ASCII strings; Line 14: round-trip assertion fails post-migration

finding.fix_hint: Replace string.getBytes() with string.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8) on line 16 and new String(bytes) with new String(bytes, StandardCharsets.UTF_8) on line 20, adding import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.
